The electrochemical reduction of CO2 to multicarbon (C2+) products offers a promising route to sustainable fuels and chemicals, while controlling the critical C-C coupling step remains a fundamental challenge.

The Cu2I2-triazine catalysts achieved a high Faradaic efficiency of 73.7% for C2+ products at a current density of -300 mA cm-2, significantly outperforming the Cu3I3-triazine counterpart in CO2 electroreduction.

evidence.title: [ASAP] Multifunctional Molecular Cages Boost Acidic CO2 Electroreduction to Ethylene

key_parameters.evidence_text: However, two intrinsic limitations, rapid reduction of Cu+ to Cu0 and fast desorption/diffusion of the *CO intermediate, severely hindered C–C coupling and thus diminish ethylene (C2H4) selectivity in acidic CO2RR.

Here, we constructed a molecular cage on the Cu2O surface by grafting cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB), which simultaneously stabilized Cu+ and restricted *CO diffusion, thereby enabling efficient CO2-to-C2H4 conver
As a result, the optimized Cu2O@CTAB catalyst delivered a high C2H4 Faradaic efficiency of 60% across 300–1100 mA cm–2 in the strongly acidic electrolyte.
As a result, the optimized Cu2O@CTAB catalyst delivered a high C2H4 Faradaic efficiency of 60% across 300–1100 mA cm–2 in the strongly acidic electrolyte.

evidence.title: [ASAP] Inverse Design of Ag–Cu Bimetallic Alloys: Tuning C1+ Selectivity during CO2 Electroreduction
[ASAP] Tailoring Dual-Functional Ionomers for Efficient CO2 Electroreduction to Ethanol

key_parameters.evidence_text: Using commercial copper nanoparticles as the catalyst, 57.3% ethanol faradaic efficiency (FE) and 29.3% cathodic energy efficiency (CEE) are achieved over PAMV-Cu based gas diffusion electrodes in bicarbonate electrolyte

Using commercial copper nanoparticles as the catalyst, 57.3% ethanol faradaic efficiency (FE) and 29.3% cathodic energy efficiency (CEE) are achieved over PAMV-Cu based gas diffusion electrodes in bicarbonate electrolyte
This performance is approximately 4 times that of the commonly used commercial Nafion ionomer under the same conditions.

This work provides a novel ionomer-engineered approach for modulating electrocatalytic reactions, which offers a robust and convenient solution for the efficient conversion of CO2 to value-added products.
